name: specforge-ai
description: Minimal VS Code extension for exploring SpecForge.AI user stories.

specForge.execution.modelProfiles.description: Named model profiles. Each profile declares its own provider and capabilities so the workflow can route phases explicitly. `codex`, `claude`, and `copilot` run natively from their local CLI. Use `openai-compatible` for endpoint-based bridge profiles.

name.description: Stable profile name used by phase assignments.

provider.description: Provider kind used by this profile. `codex`, `claude`, and `copilot` use their native local CLI. `openai-compatible` uses the configured chat/completions endpoint.

baseUrl.description: Base URL for this profile's endpoint. Required for `openai-compatible`. Omit for native CLI providers.

apiKey.description: API key for this profile's endpoint. Required for remote `openai-compatible` endpoints. Not used by native CLI execution.

model.description: Concrete model identifier exposed by this profile's endpoint. Optional for native CLI execution, which can fall back to the local provider default.

reasoningEffort.description: Optional reasoning effort override. Sent to `openai-compatible` HTTP profiles and Codex CLI profiles when supported.

repositoryAccess.description: Repository access granted to this profile. Clarification, refinement, and technical design require read. Implementation and review require read-write.

specForge.execution.phaseModels.description: Assign named model profiles to workflow phases. Any unassigned phase falls back to the default profile.

defaultProfile.description: Fallback profile used when a specific phase does not declare its own profile.

technicalDesignProfile.description: Optional profile override for technical design.

implementationProfile.description: Optional profile override for implementation. This is the place for the strongest model.

reviewProfile.description: Optional profile override for review. Falls back to the default profile when unset.

releaseApprovalProfile.description: Optional profile override for release approval.

prPreparationProfile.description: Optional profile override for PR preparation.

specForge.execution.clarificationToleran.description: How demanding clarification should be before the workflow advances to refinement. This also adjusts the temperature sent for the clarification phase.

specForge.execution.reviewTolerance.description: How demanding the review phase should be when assessing findings and pass/fail readiness. This also adjusts the temperature sent for the review phase.

specForge.execution.autoClarificationAns.description: Named profile used to auto-answer clarification questions before handing refinement/spec back to the user.

specForge.ui.enableWatcher.description: Refresh the explorer and workflow view automatically when .specs files change.

specForge.ui.notifyOnAttention.description: Show notifications when a user story becomes waiting-user, blocked, or completed.

specForge.features.enableContextSuggesti.description: Suggest repository context files during clarification using local heuristics and repo neighborhood rules.

specForge.features.requireApprovalBranch.description: Require explicit acceptance of the proposed base branch in the workflow detail before approving refinement.

specForge.features.autoClarificationAnsw.description: When clarification blocks refinement/spec, let the selected model try to answer the pending questions once with the full workflow context before handing the phase to the user.

specForge.features.autoPlayEnabled.description: Automatically resume workflow playback after manual actions such as approving refinement when the next phase can continue.

specForge.features.autoReviewEnabled.description: Automatically continue from implementation into review after implementation artifacts are generated or updated.

specForge.features.maxImplementationRevi.description: Maximum implementation attempts allowed in the implementation/review loop before automatic continuation stops.

specForge.features.destructiveRewindEnab.description: When enabled, rewind and regress delete later derived artifacts and branch metadata. When disabled, both only move the workflow state back.

specForge.features.pauseOnFailedReview.description: When enabled, automatically pause workflow playback on a failed review and show the review warning overlay for developer inspection.

specForge.features.completedUsLockOnComp.description: When enabled, completed user stories stay locked against direct rewind or artifact modification. Explicit reopen from the completed phase remains available.
